trainer.name: Wexford Stables
trainer.bio: Wexford Stables is headed by Lance O'Sullivan ONZM and Andrew Scott at Matamata. Lance is a 12-time champion jockey with 2,479 career wins, including the 1989 Japan Cup aboard Horlicks. The training partnership has produced 600+ wins.

horse.name: Prudentia
horse.story: Prudentia was bought as a yearling out of the NZ Bloodstock Select Sale. She showed early promise in the breaking-in yard — a willing attitude and a turn of foot on soft ground. She won her maiden at Tauranga as a 3-year-old and went on to claim a tough, tactical victory in Race 7 (BM65, 1300m) at Te Rapa on a Heavy 10 in April 2026.

trainer.name: Wexford Stables

horse.name: Hottathanafantasy
horse.story: A late-season 2-year-old filly showing early maturity in the breaking-in yard. Hotta's dam was a sharp sprinter over 1200m, and the filly has inherited that early speed. Currently in pre-training.

trainer.name: Stephen Gray Racing
trainer.location: Copper Belt Lodge, near Palmerston North, New Zealand
trainer.bio: Stephen Gray Racing operates from Copper Belt Lodge near Palmerston North. Owned by Stephen Gray & Kevin Gray, the stable is known for getting the best out of stayers and late-maturing types.

horse.name: First Gear
horse.story: First Gear is a 12-month lease syndication with clear campaign targets. He's a staying type who should mature into a genuine 2000m horse. His first preparation showed enough to believe there's a stakes campaign in him.

horse.name: I Stole A Manolo
horse.story: A stylish grey filly with a pedigree suggesting middle-distance strength. Currently spelling after a successful breaking-in. Next preparation will show whether she has the speed for a 2-year-old campaign.
